task_outcomes.task_1_audit_intake_step_1d: Step 1d reads monitor/integrity/report-*.json. Severity filter: only severity='moderate' triggers the explicit ISS-creation block (lines 73-103 of decider-intake.md). Creates ISS when finding doesn't match an existing open or recently-closed ISS by category+location. Runs in every decider mode (no mode-gating). DELIVERABLE: 'Step 1d reads monitor/integrity/report-*.json, filters by severity=moderate ONLY, creates ISS via internal dedup against open-issues.json + 7d-closed, runs unconditionally on every decider invocation.' Critical/major/minor severities have no concrete ISS-creation procedure — only vague 'treat critical issues as priority 1' / 'flag for immediate rebuild' guidance.
task_outcomes.task_2_git_log_step_1d_history: git log monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md since 2026-04-01 returns: ec95134 (2026-05-06, original integrity-moderate handoff gap fix — landed Step 1d's moderate-specific block), b13d162/bdc733b (2026-05-06, PROP-022 phase 2/3 — touch Step 1a/1h2, NOT Step 1d), 9db26e3 (2026-05-09, summary rename — touches summary references, NOT Step 1d), a9fbd67 (2026-05-09, PROP-025 — touches Step 1f new-WIN onboarding, NOT Step 1d), 38d40ed (2026-05-11, PROP-028 stale-assessment — touches Step 1h2 predictions, NOT Step 1d), 4ed9ccb (2026-05-15, workspace sync). DELIVERABLE: 'No relevant changes. Step 1d was last touched on 2026-05-06 (ec95134) when it was authored. Hypothesis A (code regression) is RULED OUT.'
task_outcomes.task_3_check_today_findings_vs_decider_i: Decider 2026-05-15T01-30 daily-report: 1 ISS created (ISS-2014, DOI verification — unrelated to integrity); 0 recommended_actions mentioning integrity. Decider 2026-05-15T17-20 daily-report: 0 ISSs created; 1 recommended_action mentioning integrity ('Integrity report 2026-05-15T01:16 flagged build_drift and workspace_files_at_risk — likely stale from'). DELIVERABLE: 'Decider attempted integrity intake at least once (17:20 run mentions it in recommended_actions, proving the file was read), but did NOT create any ISSs for non-moderate findings. The intake path is being READ; the ACTION-on-non-moderates step is missing from the prompt.'
task_outcomes.task_4_decider_run_history_audit: 8 decider runs audited (2026-05-13 → 2026-05-16): 0 integrity_finding-category ISSs across all 8 runs. Inflection point: never present — the pattern goes back at minimum to 2026-05-13. Pre-2026-05-13 data not fetched (15-day shallow window adequate for the directive's question). DELIVERABLE: 'Per-run integrity-derived ISS count: [13T05:0, 13T13:0, 14T01:0, 14T21:0, 15T01:0, 15T13:0, 15T17:0, 16T01:0]. No inflection point found in the 8-run window. This is at minimum an 8-run pattern, consistent with the prompt-level gap (not a recent regression).'
task_outcomes.task_5_model_class_test: Not strictly needed (Hypothesis A ruled out and Hypothesis C confirmed independent of model class), but quoted for completeness. Step 1d's intake instruction verbatim: '**Moderate findings (`severity: "moderate"`)** — must be triaged on EVERY decider run. ... For each `severity: "moderate"` finding ...'. Sonnet-readability assessment: the prompt's IF condition is rigid (severity=='moderate'), but the IF only matches one severity. On Sonnet AND Opus, both will literally evaluate the condition and skip non-moderate findings — the model-class is not the discriminator. DELIVERABLE: 'Hypothesis B (model-class compatibility) is NOT the root cause. The prompt's severity-filter is the gate; both models follow it identically. Sonnet may have additional shallowness on intake (worth a separate analysis) but the dominant bug is the prompt scope.'
task_outcomes.task_6_design_canonical_fix: See PROP-037-decider-integrity-intake-handoff.json. Recommendation: Option D-refined (hybrid path-pin + explicit severity-agnostic loop, triggered on integrity's already-emitted tracked_under==null signal). Rationale: model-class-resilient (rigid IF/THEN, explicit field extraction), respects existing integrity-side dedup work, generalizes to every severity. Answers to all four design_questions provided in PROP body.
task_outcomes.task_7_counter_argument: 5 counter-arguments steelmanned + rebutted in PROP-037 counter_argument_pass section: (1) integrity-as-operator-attention; (2) operator-handles-in-45min; (3) revert-to-Opus; (4) severity-proliferation noise; (5) overall_status:fail already says priority-1.

findings.description: Decider's Step 1d in decider-intake.md (L64-L103) only creates ISSs for severity='moderate' integrity findings. Critical/major/minor findings fall back to vague non-actionable guidance and produce zero ISSs in practice (audited across 8 decider runs 2026-05-13 → 2026-05-16). Despite integrity emitting tracked_under==null on every untracked finding regardless of severity, decider treats severity as the trigger instead of tracked_under. The 2026-05-15 case forced operator to hand-apply 5 findings at 05:00-05:45Z this morning (commits c7b1274 + ecdbcb2).
findings.evidence: PROP-037 evidence_log section; 8 daily-reports audited; structure-integrity.md L565 quoted to show the contract integrity claims is being honored.
findings.recommended_action: Operator review PROP-037; on approval, tinker (or operator) applies the prompt edit in a single commit. Verification path documented in PROP-037 verification_pattern section.

findings.description: Meta-pattern: both DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 (analyst summary-write gap → PROP-036) and DIRECTIVE-20260515-001 (this directive → PROP-037) are 'contract failures' — the agents do exactly what their prompts say; the prompts are wrong. The read-side and write-side of a handoff must use the SAME signal. For PROP-036: analyst writes summary at canonical path ↔ tinker pipeline-health reads summary mtime. For PROP-037: integrity emits tracked_under ↔ decider triggers on tracked_under==null. Both fixes restore symmetry on the consuming side. A future PROP on 'handoff-contract symmetry as a pipeline invariant' is worth considering — there are likely other handoffs that are asymmetric and waiting to fail.
findings.recommended_action: Defer to a future tinker Mode-3 pass. Not blocking PROP-036 or PROP-037.

backlog_trend.interpretation: Pipeline backlog is in HEALTHY shape — open_issues_total=57 well under 200 moderate threshold, net_velocity_7d=+141 (closing way more than creating, PROP-026 burndown effective), assigned-analyst=44 below 50 moderate threshold. age_ge_14d=37 is elevated but the corresponding age_ge_30d=1 is fine — the 14-30d cohort is large but turning over. No tier fired. oldest_open_age_days=null because no issues have status='open' (all in 'assigned-analyst' or other states); not a metric defect — just reflects the open_status_count=0.
backlog_trend.baby_drain_observation: baby_drain_count_7d=0 — possibly a schema-match issue (expansion-tracker.json may not have authored_by/claimed_by fields named exactly that, or completed_at may be on a different field). Not blocking. Worth a future calibration pass in a Mode-3 run.
